name: Lowfat Chicken Stock
recipeIngredient: 12 1/3 cups canned chicken broth (2-49 1/2 oz. cans)
3 to 3 1/2 lbs chicken legs and thighs, skinned, fat removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Discard the fat from the top of the canned chicken broth.
2. Heat a very large stockpot. If you don't have a very large stockpot, divide the ingredients and make the stock in two stockpots.
3. Remove from heat and spray with vegetable oil spray.
4. Toss in the onion and carrot, lower the heat, and cover the pot. Cook over Medium-High, stirring frequently, until the chicken is browned on both sides (about 5 minutes).
5. Pour in the chicken broth and water. Add celery and bay leaves; bring to a boil.
6. Boil for 5 minutes. Skim off foam as it accumulates and discard.
7. Lower the heat to simmer and add thyme. Simmer, covered, for 2 hours. Add water if necessary to keep the chicken covered with liquid.
8. Remove the pot from heat; let cool. Remove the chicken and allow to cool, then pick the meat from the bones. Discard vegetables and bay leaves.
9. Cool the stock to room temperature, cover, and refrigerate overnight.
10. Lift any congealed fat and discard. Store in the refrigerator for 2-3 days or freeze for longer storage.
Makes 20-24 cups.
